summary: Executed full backend pytest regression and frontend desktop/mobile integration checks on public URL. Core flows pass; one requirement mismatch and one mobile layout concern remain.

ui_bugs.issue: Content count does not match requested scope: page shows 8 creative services and 9 AI/technology services, but requirement asks for 9 creative and 10 AI/technology services.

issues: Observed horizontal overflow risk on mobile check (document body width 411px vs viewport 390px).

action_items: Align Services data/content with requested counts: 9 creative + 10 AI/technology services in backend content and rendered UI.
Investigate and fix mobile horizontal overflow source to ensure no sideways scrolling on small screens.
Retest Services counts and mobile layout after fixes.
critical_code_review_comments: Requirement-to-content drift exists between requested service counts and API/UI data model values.
Test suites previously depended on environment variable availability only; tests were updated to reliably resolve public base URL from frontend .env for regression continuity.
seed_data_creation: Created test contact/newsletter records via API during regression execution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression is green (14/14). Desktop UI checks passed across Home/Services/Ecosystem/Learning/Music/Showcase/Blog/FAQ/Contact with working quick actions and scroll-to-top navigation. Remaining checks: required services count mismatch (8/9 vs expected 9/10) and mobile overflow signal.
rca of the issue: RCA-1: Services mismatch is content/schema-level, not selector issue. Current DEFAULT_SITE_CONTENT and fallback content define only 8 creative and 9 technology entries; UI faithfully renders those counts. Mitigation: add missing items in backend site content defaults (and optional frontend fallback sync). RCA-2: Mobile overflow likely caused by at least one fixed/wide element exceeding viewport width in rendered page context; validate by identifying element with width > viewport during mobile run and constrain with responsive rules.
